title: Associative Arrays
description: Use named keys to store key-value pairs.

slides.title: Key-Value Pairs
slides.content:
# Associative Arrays

In associative arrays, you assign specific keys (usually strings) to values. It's like a dictionary or a map.

```php
$ages = [
    "Alice" => 25,
    "Bob"   => 30,
    "Charlie" => 22
];
```

Access values using their key:

```php
echo $ages["Alice"]; // 25
```

slides.title: Working with Associative Arrays

quizData.question: How do you access the value with key 'color' in an associative array `$item`?
quizData.explanation: Use square brackets with the key inside quotes: `$item['color']`.
